name: Kwirk
description:
Kwirk is a single-screen puzzle game where the player(s) navigate a maze-like structure to reach the exit. The core mechanic revolves around manipulating rotatable face blocks, each featuring arrows pointing in four directions. These arrows determine the direction in which a character will move when pushed onto the block. The mazes are populated with various obstacles, including walls that block movement and holes that the character must avoid. 

The player controls one or more characters (either individually or in a coordinated manner) to solve the puzzles. The goal is to strategically push these face blocks to create a pathway for the character(s) to reach the level exit. Some levels require multiple characters to reach separate exits simultaneously. Certain blocks may also trigger events, such as opening gates or activating elevators that can transport the character to different parts of the level. Level complexity increases as the player progresses, introducing new types of obstacles and more intricate maze designs. The puzzles require careful planning and spatial reasoning to solve effectively.

controls.arrow_up: Move currently selected character up one space
controls.arrow_down: Move currently selected character down one space
controls.arrow_left: Move currently selected character left one space
controls.arrow_right: Move currently selected character right one space
controls.w: Switch to controlling the character above the current character
controls.s: Switch to controlling the character below the current character
controls.a: Switch to controlling the character to the left of the current character
controls.d: Switch to controlling the character to the right of the current character
controls.space: Wait one turn; no character moves
controls.shift: Rotate the currently selected face block clockwise (if standing on a rotatable face block)
controls.q: Rotate the currently selected face block counter-clockwise (if standing on a rotatable face block)
